description: Add PluginEntry struct to mod.rs for carrying plugin name + description to registrar
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs
Add a new `PluginEntry` struct with `name: String` and `description: Option<String>` fields
Derive Debug and Clone on the struct
Add doc comments explaining this is data needed to register a plugin in marketplace.json
Verify the struct is public (`pub struct`) so registrar.rs can access it
Run `cargo build --workspace` to confirm compilation

description: Update registrar::register_plugins signature to accept &[PluginEntry] instead of &[String]
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/migrate/registrar.rs
Import `super::PluginEntry` at the top of the file
Change function signature from `plugin_names: &[String]` to `entries: &[PluginEntry]`
Change the early-return guard from `plugin_names.is_empty()` to `entries.is_empty()`
Update the loop to iterate over `entries` instead of `plugin_names`
In the `already_registered` check, use `entry.name.as_str()` instead of `name.as_str()`
Extract description with fallback: `entry.description.as_deref().unwrap_or("Migrated from .claude/ configuration")`
Use the extracted description in the `serde_json::json!` macro instead of the hardcoded string
Use `format!("./{}", entry.name)` for the source field
Run `cargo build --workspace` to confirm compilation (expect errors at call sites — those are fixed in next features)

description: Update migrate_single_source in mod.rs to collect PluginEntry with descriptions and pass to registrar
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs, locate `migrate_single_source` function (~line 241)
Change `let mut registered_names: Vec<String>` to `let mut registered_entries: Vec<PluginEntry>`

In the artifact loop, after `emitter::emit_plugin()` returns `plugin_name`, push a `PluginEntry { name: plugin_name, description: artifact.metadata.description.clone() }` instead of just `plugin_name`
Update the `registrar::register_plugins(ai_dir, &registered_entries, fs)?` call
Update the `MarketplaceRegistered` action loop to iterate `&registered_entries` and use `entry.name`
Run `cargo build --workspace` to confirm compilation

description: Update migrate_recursive in mod.rs to collect PluginEntry with descriptions and pass to registrar
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs, locate `migrate_recursive` function (~line 302)
Change `let mut registered_names: Vec<String>` to `let mut registered_entries: Vec<PluginEntry>`
In the emission results collection loop, build `PluginEntry` with: name from result, description from `plan.artifacts.first().and_then(|a| a.metadata.description.clone())`
This requires capturing the plan reference alongside the emission result — adjust the parallel emission to return `(actions, name, Option<String>)` or capture description before parallel emission
Update the `registrar::register_plugins(ai_dir, &registered_entries, fs)?` call
Update the `MarketplaceRegistered` action loop to iterate `&registered_entries` and use `entry.name`
Run `cargo build --workspace` to confirm compilation

description: Update existing registrar unit tests to use PluginEntry instead of String
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/migrate/registrar.rs, locate #[cfg(test)] module (~line 49)
In `register_appends_to_empty_plugins_array`: change `vec!["deploy".to_string(), ...]` to `vec![PluginEntry { name: "deploy".to_string(), description: None }, ...]`
In `register_appends_alongside_existing`: change names vec to PluginEntry vec with `description: None`
In `register_skips_already_registered`: change names vec to PluginEntry vec with `description: None`
In `register_preserves_marketplace_metadata`: change names vec to PluginEntry vec with `description: None`
Run `cargo test --workspace` to confirm all existing tests still pass

description: Add new registrar unit tests verifying description propagation
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/migrate/registrar.rs tests module
Add test `register_uses_entry_description`: create PluginEntry with `description: Some("Deploy app".to_string())`, register it, parse written JSON, assert description equals "Deploy app"
Add test `register_uses_fallback_when_no_description`: create PluginEntry with `description: None`, register it, parse written JSON, assert description equals "Migrated from .claude/ configuration"
Add test `register_mixed_descriptions`: create two entries — one with `Some("My desc")` and one with `None` — register both, parse written JSON, verify each plugin has the correct description
Run `cargo test --workspace` to confirm all new tests pass

description: Update migrate_full_flow unit test to verify marketplace.json descriptions match plugin.json
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs, locate `migrate_full_flow` test (~line 588)
After asserting action counts, read the written marketplace.json from `MockFs`
Parse the marketplace.json as serde_json::Value
Find the 'deploy' plugin entry and assert its description is "Deploy app" (matching SKILL.md frontmatter)
Find the 'review' plugin entry and assert its description is "Migrated from .claude/ configuration" (command has no frontmatter description in the test fixture)
Run `cargo test --workspace` to confirm test passes

description: Add E2E test verifying marketplace description matches plugin.json description after migrate
steps: Open crates/aipm/tests/migrate_e2e.rs
Add test `migrate_marketplace_description_matches_plugin_json`
Create a temp directory with .ai/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json (empty plugins array) and .claude/skills/deploy/SKILL.md with frontmatter containing `description: Deploy the application`
Run the migrate CLI command (or call the library directly)
Read the emitted .ai/deploy/.claude-plugin/plugin.json and parse it
Read the updated .ai/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json and parse it
Find the 'deploy' entry in marketplace.json plugins array
Assert that marketplace description equals plugin.json description
Run `cargo test --workspace` to confirm the E2E test passes

description: Run full build, clippy, fmt, and coverage checks to verify all quality gates pass
steps: Run `cargo build --workspace` — zero errors
Run `cargo test --workspace` — all tests pass
Run `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` — zero warnings
Run `cargo fmt --check` — no formatting issues
Run coverage commands per CLAUDE.md: `cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace`, then collect workspace + doctest coverage, then verify >= 89% branch coverage
Fix any issues found until all gates pass
